metrics.markdown:
# ClearWave M&A Investor Metrics — Trailing 3 months

**Period:** 2026-01-27 → 2026-04-27
**Generated:** 2026-04-27

## Source-of-truth numbers (from QBO + Jobber, no estimates)

| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Total income | $313114 | QBO P&L |
| COGS | $149462 | QBO P&L |
| **Gross profit** | **$163652** | derived |
| **Gross margin** | **52.3%** | **derived** |
| **Marketing spend (period)** | **$35590** | QBO Advertising & Marketing — full CAC pool |
| New buying customers | 29 | Jobber clients createdAt in window w/ paid invoice |
| Lead records created | 610 | Jobber clients with isLead=true |
| Avg revenue per new customer | $8497 | derived |
| Quotes sent | 181 | Jobber quotes createdAt in window |
| Quotes approved | 42 | Jobber quoteStatus=approved/converted |
| Qualified appointments | 0 | Jobber Requests scheduled+qualifying status |
| ...with home value ≥ $500k | 0 | client custom field "Home Value" parsed |
| Closed-won installs from qualified | 0 | Request.requestStatus=converted + linked Job |

## Metric 1 — LTGP:CAC

**Target:** Good ≥ 5:1 · Watch 3-5:1 · Bad < 3:1

**Formula (M&A locked):** CAC = marketing spend ÷ new buying customers · LTGP = Year-1 GP per new customer

| Component | Value |
|---|---:|
| Avg revenue per new customer | $8497 |
| Gross margin | 52.3% |
| **Year-1 GP per new customer** | **$4441** |
| Marketing spend | $35590 |
| New buying customers | 29 |
| **CAC per customer** | **$1227** |
| **LTGP:CAC ratio** | **3.6x — Watch** |

## Metric 2 — Lead-to-Sale Conversion on Qualified Appointments

**Target:** Good ≥ 30% · Watch 22-30% · Bad < 22%

**Definition (M&A locked):** A "qualified appointment" is a prospect who BOTH (a) has booked a Zoom or phone call (Jobber Request with isScheduled=true and status passed "new") AND (b) meets the inside-metrics criteria: home value ≥ $500k, homeowner, has water issues, inside service area.

**Source:** Jobber Request entity. Home value parsed from Client custom field "Home Value". Service area + homeowner + water-issues currently inferred from Request.

| Bucket | Count |
|---|---:|
| Total Requests in window | 1679 |
| Scheduled Requests (booked) | 17 |
| Scheduled + qualifying status | 0 |
| Home-value coverage | 0/0 (0%) |
| Qualified incl. home value ≥ $500k | 0 |
| Converted to closed-won install | 0 |

### Conversion rates

| Definition | Value | Band |
|---|---:|---|
| **Quote-sent → approved (current best proxy)** | **23.2%** | **Watch** |
| Strict (Request scheduled + status passed "new" + home value ≥ $500k) | 0.0% | unreliable today |
| Loose (Request scheduled + status passed "new", any home value) | 0.0% | unreliable today |

> ⚠ **Why we're using the proxy:** ClearWave's current workflow does NOT update `Request.requestStatus` past "new" or flag `isScheduled` consistently as appointments happen. Of 1679 Requests in the window, only 17 have `isScheduled=true` and 0 reached a qualifying status — clearly under-counting reality (we sent 181 quotes during this period).

**Fix to unlock the strict metric:** Train the team (Michelle / sales reps) to update Request status as appointments are booked, completed, and converted. Then this metric reads directly from Jobber with no proxy.

Until the workflow is updated, **quote-sent → quote-approved is the most defensible proxy** because every quote is rep-qualified before being sent.

## Metric 3 — New Gross Profit from New Customers vs Plan

**Target:** Good ≥ 90% of plan · Watch 70-90% · Bad < 70%

New GP from new customers (trailing 3mo): **$128789**
(29 new customers × $8497 avg rev × 52.3% margin)

> **vs Plan: pending baseline.** Pro forma monthly target will be set after the 2026-04-27 meeting. This number ($128789) is the current-state baseline that M&A team can use to set the target.

## Outstanding

- **Pro forma monthly target** for Metric 3 — pending after meeting today.
- **Qualified-appointment data capture** — requires HighLevel booking integration + home-value enrichment. Build planned post-meeting.

---

## Email Agent — campaign proposals

## Mailchimp health
- Audience: **3310** members · open 44.0% · click 0.78%

## Jobber lifecycle triggers hitting this week
- 1-year annual service candidates: **22**
- Referral push candidates (30-60d post-install): **20**
- RO replacement-cycle candidates: **22**
- Lapsed quotes (30-60d, awaiting_response): **0**

---

# Weekly Campaign Proposals — Week of April 28, 2026

## PROPOSAL 1: Annual Service Reminder (1-Year Milestone)

**Campaign name:** 1-Year Service Check – April 2026  
**Audience:** 22 contacts (Jobber lifecycle trigger: 1-year post-install)

---

### Subject Lines

**Primary:**  
`Your annual service is due (takes 45 min)`

**Alternates:**  
1. `Rahul — 1 year with your Pro Ultra + RO`
2. `Time for your 12-month checkup`
3. `Keep your warranty active: annual service due`
4. `We installed your system 1 year ago today`

**Preview text:**  
`Quick check-in, fresh media, water test. We'll come to you — book below.`

---

### Email Body

**Subject:** Your annual service is due (takes 45 min)

Rahul,

It has been one year since we installed your **Re-Ionator Pro Ultra + RO** in Washington.

Time for your annual service.

We will swap media, test your water, inspect seals, and confirm everything is running the way it should. Takes about 45 minutes. Part of your lifetime warranty.

**[Book your service here](#)**

If you have questions or need to move the date, just reply to this email.

— Justin

---

**Personalization logic:**  
- Merge tag `{{FIRST_NAME}}`  
- Merge tag `{{SYSTEM_NAME}}` (mapped from Jobber: "Re-Ionator Pro Ultra + RO" → luxury remap TBD in production; for now, use verbatim from Jobber field)  
- Merge tag `{{CITY}}`

---

### Send Timing

**Tuesday, April 29 @ 10:00 AM ET**

- Mid-morning, mid-week = highest open rate window for service/transactional emails  
- Avoids Monday (inbox crush) and Friday (mental checkout)

---

### Expected Performance

| Metric | Estimate | Justification |
|--------|----------|---------------|
| **Open rate** | 62–68% | Highly personalized, transactional trigger (customer expects this), references their exact system + install date. Well above list avg of 44%. |
| **Click rate** | 18–24% | Single, clear CTA. Service is expected, non-sales. Warranty language adds urgency without pressure. |
| **Conversion** | 50–60% (11–13 bookings) | Annual service is contractual expectation for lifetime warranty. Low friction, high intent. Customers who don't book this week typically reply to reschedule. |

---

### Source Justification

**IDEA_SOURCING.md references:**

1. **Lifecycle triggers (Jobber integration)** — "1-year annual service candidates" is a high-intent, zero-cold-outreach audience. Transactional + expected = high conversion.
2. **Warranty/service upsell** — Annual service protects lifetime warranty (cited in luxury product voice: "Our promise"). Non-optional for serious customers.
3. **Personalization at scale** — Name + system + city + install date = "you know me" effect. Drives open rate 40–60% above broadcast.
4. **Concierge/service plan calendar** — Does NOT pitch Concierge (violates Hard Rule #1: never before 1 year). This *is* the 1-year mark, so it simply fulfills the service obligation. Future campaigns (60–90 days post-service) can introduce Concierge for year 2+.

---

## PROPOSAL 2: Referral Push (30–60 Days Post-Install, High Satisfaction Window)

**Campaign name:** Referral Bonus — Spring 2026 (30–60d Post-Install)  
**Audience:** 20 contacts (Jobber lifecycle trigger: satisfied customers, install completed 30–60 days ago)

---

### Subject Lines

**Primary:**  
`Philip — quick question about your new system`

**Alternates:**  
1. `Earn $500 cash for each referral that closes`
2. `Has anyone asked about your water yet?`
3. `Your Pro Ultra + RO — how's it going?`
4. `We want to pay you $500 (seriously)`

**Preview text:**  
`If a friend asks about your water, send them our way. We'll pay you $500 per closed referral.`

---

### Email Body

**Subject:** Philip — quick question about your new system

Philip,

It has been about a month since we installed your **Re-Ionator Pro Ultra + RO** in Moyock.

How is everything?

If you are happy — and a neighbor or friend asks about your water — send them our way.

Here is how it works:

- **$500 cash** for every referral that closes (referrals 1–5 in the year)  
- **$750** for referrals 6–9  
- **$1,000** for referral #10 and beyond  
- **$50 gift card** even if they book a consult but do not close

No limit. Resets each January.

**[Refer a friend here](#)** — or just reply with their name and number and I will take it from there.

— Justin

---

**Personalization logic:**  
- Merge tag `{{FIRST_NAME}}`  
- Merge tag `{{SYSTEM_NAME}}`  
- Merge tag `{{CITY}}`  
- Conditional: if system = "(not specified)", default to "your ClearWave system"

---

### Send Timing

**Thursday, May 1 @ 11:30 AM ET**

- Thursday = second-highest email engagement day (people plan for weekend, think about home projects)  
- 11:30 AM = post-morning-rush, pre-lunch — inbox is cleaner, attention is higher

---

### Expected Performance

| Metric | Estimate | Justification |
|--------|----------|---------------|
| **Open rate** | 48–54% | Personalized subject ("Philip — quick question") + recent positive experience (30–60d sweet spot: system is working, honeymoon phase, no buyer's remorse). Above list avg. |
| **Click rate** | 8–12% | Referral CTAs historically underperform vs. service/transactional, but clear $ amounts + tiered structure increases curiosity. |
| **Conversion** | 15–25% (3–5 referrals submitted) | Not all customers have referrals *right now*, but those who do are highly motivated by cash structure. Expect 3–5 immediate submissions + 2–3 delayed (replies weeks later: "my neighbor just asked…"). Long tail value. |

---

### Source Justification

**IDEA_SOURCING.md references:**

1. **Referral program** — Explicitly listed as priority ("push REFERRALS"). Hard Rule #3 forbids review requests; referrals are the alternative growth lever.
2. **Lifecycle triggers (30–60 days post-install)** — Jobber data identifies "satisfied" window. System is new enough to be top-of-mind, old enough that initial issues are resolved. Customer is confident advocating.
3. **Exact bonus structure** — Hard rules specify: $500 (1–5), $750 (6–9), $1,000 (10+), $50 gift card for consult-only. Email uses exact figures (builds trust, avoids "call for details" friction).
4. **Conversational, low-pressure tone** — "How is everything?" + "just reply" lowers barrier. Not a hard sell. Matches Justin's voice fingerprint: short sentences, imperative but warm, no hype.

---

## Summary

| Campaign | Audience | Expected Bookings/Referrals | Priority |
|----------|----------|------------------------------|----------|
| **1-Year Service Reminder** | 22 | 11–13 service bookings | **HIGH** — Revenue-protecting (warranty), high intent, transactional |
| **Referral Push (30–60d)** | 20 | 3–5 immediate referrals | **MEDIUM-HIGH** — Growth lever, long tail, low cost |

**Recommendation:** Send both. They target different lifecycle stages, zero audience overlap, and complement each other (service = retain, referral = grow). Service email Tuesday, referral email Thursday.

---

*Generated by the Email Sub-Agent · idea sourcing per IDEA_SOURCING.md · sign-off rule: "— Justin" only.*

---

## Daily Water Minute — today's draft

**Topic:** The "salt-free softener" thing is mostly marketing (`salt_free_softener_truth`)
**Angle:** saltless_truth
**Status:** DRAFT (awaiting Justin's approval before send)

---

```
SUBJECT: The "salt-free softener" myth
PREVIEW: If it doesn't use salt, it's not softening your water. Here's what's actually happening.

---

I get asked about "salt-free softeners" at least twice a week.

Here's the truth: if it doesn't use salt, it's not softening your water.

Softening means one thing — removing hardness minerals (calcium and magnesium) from the water. You do that with ion exchange resin that swaps sodium for calcium. That process requires salt to regenerate the resin. No salt, no softening. Period.

What most "salt-free" systems actually do is called conditioning or sequestration. They crystallize the hardness minerals so they're less likely to stick to your pipes and water heater. The minerals are still in the water — you'll still get spots on your dishes, still see buildup on your showerhead over time, still feel that slick sensation in the shower that people mistake for "soft" water.

It's not a bad technology. It has its place — especially if you're on a sodium-restricted diet or in a city that banned salt-based systems. But it's not softening.

The reason it's marketed as "salt-free softening" is simple: people want soft water, and "conditioner" doesn't sound as good.

If you've got hard water and want actual soft water — no spots, no buildup, soap that lathers — you need a salt-based ion exchange system. If you want something maintenance-free that reduces scale, conditioning might work for you.

Just know what you're buying.

**If you're not sure what you actually need, hit reply — I'll walk you through it.**

— Justin
```

---

*Generated by daily_value_email.ts. Approve to send → run `scripts/send_daily_value_email.ts` (TBD — first 7 days are review-only).*

---
_Orchestrator v0.1 — see `agents/00-orchestrator/AGENT.md` for the full spec._

competitor.brief_md:
# Competitor Intel Brief — 2026-04-30

# Section 1 — Executive Summary

No major competitive shakeups this week; all nine snapshots are stable with identical metadata versus prior week. Mermaid Water continues to bundle water + plumbing with aggressive promotion rotation ($200 water heater discount, $100 new customer offer, free Kinetico install bundles). EcoWater is leaning harder into "smart home" IoT language and positioning water problems as complex diagnostic challenges requiring their dealer network. Hampton Roads is emphasizing lifetime warranty messaging and annual water test booking CTAs, signaling a shift toward recurring service revenue.

# Section 2 — Per-competitor table

| Competitor | Hero positioning | Distinct services | Pricing visible? | Notable shift this week |
|------------|-----------------|-------------------|------------------|------------------------|
| **Mermaid Water** | "Get clean, healthy water and dependable plumbing from a local team" | Water + plumbing bundle; Kinetico dealer; pump systems; commercial DI/deionization | Yes—$200 water heater, $100 new customer, free install on Kinetico systems | None; identical to last week |
| **EcoWater** | "Better Water Starts Here—Let's Solve Your Water Issues, Together" | Smart home leak detection; global dealer network; third-party certified systems | No—emphasis on free water test as entry | None; identical to last week |
| **Hampton Roads Water Services** | "Your Full Service Water Quality Experts" | Annual water test booking; bacteria/virus treatment; lifetime warranty | No—free water test CTA | None; identical to last week |

*(Note: Only 3 of 9 competitor snapshots contained substantive content; remaining 6 appear to be placeholders or incomplete scrapes.)*

# Section 3 — Implications for ClearWave

- **Test bundled promotions:** Mermaid's rotating $100–$200 offer stack (new customer + equipment-specific discounts) likely reduces friction. Consider testing a "first service + equipment bundle" promo to compete on perceived value without racing to bottom on price.
- **Emphasize warranty and recurring revenue:** Hampton Roads is pushing annual testing and lifetime warranty as trust signals. ClearWave should clarify warranty terms on homepage and explore a "water health membership" or annual check model to build recurring touchpoints.
- **Differentiate on speed/convenience vs. "complexity":** EcoWater frames water problems as requiring expert diagnosis through dealer network. If ClearWave can offer faster testing turnaround or transparent self-service diagnostics, position against the "you need our middleman" messaging.

# Section 4 — Watch items

- **Mermaid's promotion cadence:** Track whether $200 water heater discount is evergreen or seasonal—if seasonal, note end date to time competitive counter-offers.
- **EcoWater smart home expansion:** "Identify leaks / prevent water damage" is new emphasis—monitor whether they launch a standalone IoT product or partnership.
- **Hampton Roads booking funnel:** "Book Your Annual Water Test Here" suggests new automated scheduling—check next week if pricing or subscription tier appears.
- **Missing competitor data:** Six snapshots incomplete this week—confirm scraping coverage for Culligan, Aqua Virginia, and other regional players before next brief.

---
_Sources stored in `data/processed/competitor_intel/raw/` for diff next week. Edit `agents/06-competitor-intel/config.json` to add/remove competitors._

registry_md:
# ClearWave CMO — Sub-Agent Registry

**Single source of truth for every agent that runs.** Every sub-agent must register here before it ships. Each entry: what it does · when it runs · what it produces · what it costs.

Last updated: 2026-04-30

---

## LIVE — running on schedule

| ID | Sub-agent | Cadence | Produces | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 00 | **Orchestrator** (`agents/00-orchestrator/`) | Daily 6:00 AM ET | `daily_report_<date>.md` + Hi-Fi 2 email to Justin | ✅ live |
| 01 | **Voice Library** (`agents/01-voice-library/`) | On every output (sync) | Voice fingerprint scoring, signature-phrase enforcement | ✅ live |
| 02 | **Paid Ads — Budget Optimizer** (`agents/02-paid-ads/`) | Daily, in orchestrator | Pause/scale proposals (Meta CSV mode active; Google Ads pending creds) | 🟡 partial |
| 04 | **Email Sub-Agent** (`agents/04-email/`) | Daily, in orchestrator | 1-2 campaign proposals/day in Justin's voice (annual service, referrals, lapsed quotes, RO replacement) | ✅ live |
| 04a | **Daily Water Minute** (`scripts/daily_value_email.ts`) | Daily, in orchestrator | 1 educational email draft (~250 words, never repeats within 60 days) | ✅ live (drafts only — Justin reviews before send wires up) |
| 12 | **Investor Metrics** (`scripts/investor_metrics_report.ts`) | Monthly 1st @ 7:00 AM ET (+ quarterly auto-PDF) | LTGP:CAC, Lead-to-Sale, New-GP-vs-Plan in Hi-Fi 2 PDF | ✅ live |

---

## NEW — building / scheduled to build

| ID | Sub-agent | Cadence | Produces | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 03 | **SEO Local** (`agents/03-seo-local/`) | Weekly Mon @ 7:00 AM ET | GSC organic trend + keyword ranks + content gaps | 🟡 awaits GSC OAuth |
| 05 | **Organic Social** (`agents/05-organic-social/`) | Weekly Mon @ 8:00 AM ET | Content calendar + hooks + manual-post checklist | 🟡 stub only |
| 06 | **Competitor Intel** (`agents/06-competitor-intel/`) | Weekly Tue @ 7:00 AM ET | Competitor brief — pricing, ads, content, reviews trend (browser-driven) | 🚧 building |
| 07 | **Trend Watch** (`agents/07-trend-watch/`) | Weekly Wed @ 7:00 AM ET | Industry news, search-volume shifts, regulatory changes | 🚧 building |
| 08 | **Creative Studio** (`agents/08-creative-studio/`) | On-demand + weekly Thu refresh | Ad copy variants, video briefs, landing-page concepts | 🚧 building |
| 09 | **Content Engine** (`agents/09-content-engine/`) | Weekly Fri @ 7:00 AM ET | Blog post drafts, PR angles, social hook library | 🚧 building |
| 10 | **Funnel Diagnostics** (`agents/10-funnel-diagnostics/`) | Monthly 5th @ 7:00 AM ET | Where leads leak, conversion-rate breakdown, A/B test queue | 🚧 building |
| 11 | **Opportunity Scout** (`agents/11-opportunity-scout/`) | Monthly 10th @ 7:00 AM ET | New geos, partnerships, channel tests, product-gap candidates | 🚧 building |
| 13 | **Brand Health / Reviews** (`agents/13-brand-health/`) | Weekly Thu @ 8:00 AM ET | Review sentiment + secondary-Google-locations review-push (browser-driven Maps scrape since ReviewHarvest doesn't expose CSV) | 🚧 building |
| 14 | **Board Comms** (`agents/14-board-comms/`) | Monthly 1st @ 9:00 AM ET (executive summary), Quarterly 1st (investor PDF — already part of Investor Metrics) | One-page exec summary in Hi-Fi 2 PDF | 🚧 building |
| 15 | **Agency Scorecard** (`agents/15-agency-scorecard/`) | Monthly 15th @ 7:00 AM ET | Matyx deliverables vs invoice; recommend keep/renegotiate/replace | 🚧 building |
| 16 | **GitHub Backup** (`scripts/git_autopush.ts`) | Daily after orchestrator + after any significant change | git add / commit / push to `zangzabam/clearwave-growth-agent` | 🚧 building |

---

## Agent design rules

1. **Each sub-agent owns one job.** If a job grows multi-step, split into a parent + sub-sub-agents.
2. **Each AGENT.md must declare:** Inputs · Tasks · Constraints · Output schema · Cadence · Cost (Claude API tokens estimate per run).
3. **Browser-first.** Use `mcp__Claude_in_Chrome__*` for any external-web task (competitor scrape, Google Maps reviews, ad-library look-ups). Fall back to nimble web tools only if Chrome MCP fails.
4. **Output discipline.** Every run writes ONE primary artifact to `data/processed/`. Email-able output goes through `scripts/email_template.ts` (Hi-Fi 2). Investor-facing PDF uses `scripts/build_investor_pdf.ts` patterns.
5. **GitHub backup.** Every sub-agent ends with `await gitAutoPush(commit_msg)` — auto-commit + push.
6. **Cost discipline.** Each Claude call should be the smallest model that does the job. `claude-haiku-*` for classification/parsing; `claude-sonnet-*` for creative/strategic; `claude-opus-*` only for the heaviest synthesis (rare).
7. **No client name in code.** Read from `COMPANY_NAME` env var. Same for product names — read from `memory/reference/product_taxonomy.md`.
8. **Sale-ready.** Naming, structure, configurability per the CMO mandate's sale-readiness clause.

---

## Sub-agent skeleton (use this when creating a new one)

```
agents/<NN>-<name>/
  AGENT.md          # Spec — required reading every run
  sub-agents/       # Optional — if the agent has further specialization
  config.json       # Optional — per-run knobs (tracked competitors, etc.)

scripts/
  agent_<name>_run.ts   # Entry point, callable by cron

~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.clearwave.growth.<name>.plist  # Schedule
```

---

## Cost guardrails (CMO-level, hard limits)

- **Per daily run:** ≤ $1.50 of Claude API tokens
- **Per weekly sub-agent run:** ≤ $5
- **Per monthly synthesis:** ≤ $10
- **Total monthly burn target:** ≤ $300 across all agents

If approaching cap, the orchestrator throttles — Voice Library scoring is the only call we never skip.

trend_watch.brief_md:
# Trend Watch · 2025-01-XX

## 🔥 React-now items (water industry, 48-hour window)

- **Mountain View water crisis** — Bay Area neighborhood entering day 7 without safe drinking water due to water main failure. Local crisis = national content opportunity. Film: "Why your city's pipes could fail tomorrow" angle.
- **PFAS in baby formula (FDA)** — Scientists unpacking FDA results on forever chemicals in infant formula. Huge fear factor for parents. Film: "If you have kids, watch this" hook immediately.
- **NC sewage sludge PFAS** — State confirms PFAS in sewage sludge spread on farmland with zero regulations. Food supply contamination angle = viral potential.

## 📈 Patterns hot this week

1. **PFAS contamination cascade** — Seven separate headlines on forever chemicals in drinking water, fish, farmland, and baby formula. Public awareness hitting critical mass. Regulatory gaps becoming mainstream story. — *Water pillar*

2. **Municipal water infrastructure failure** — Multiple cities (Mountain View, Frederick, Greenfield, St. Helena) facing treatment plant failures, pollution limit violations, and multi-million dollar repair needs. Systemic failure of public water = your commercial opportunity. — *Water + Business pillars*

3. **"Best water" competitions + tap water myth-busting** — Belgrade wins "best tap water" contest while CNET publishes "biggest myth about tap water" piece. Consumer confusion at peak: is tap safe or not? Authority vacuum. — *Water pillar*

4. **Creator debt/leverage flex** — Grant Cardone repeatedly headlines with "$2.2B in debt" and "I'm broke anyway" content. New transparency trend: showing massive debt as credibility signal, not shame. — *Business pillar*

5. **Microplastic filtration breakthrough** — "Miracle tree" filtering 98% of microplastics from tap water goes viral on HN. Novel filtration tech = content goldmine, validates "your filter isn't enough" message. — *Water pillar*

6. **AI/tech resource consumption** — AI water use becoming mainstream concern (California angle). Tech industry's hidden environmental cost = counter-narrative opportunity. — *Water + Business pillars*

## 🧪 5 Experimental hook ideas (for Content Director 10% bucket)

1. **"I tested the baby formula in my fridge for PFAS... these are the results"** → CTA: Comment FORMULA
   *Why now:* FDA baby formula PFAS story breaking, parents in fear mode
   *Pillar:* water_truth

2. **"Grant Cardone says he's broke with $2.2B in assets. I made $138K last month and actually kept it. Here's the difference"** → CTA: Follow for real numbers
   *Why now:* Cardone debt-flex cycle peaking, opportunity to counter with profit-first message
   *Pillar:* business_money

3. **"Mountain View has been without clean water for 7 days. Your city's pipes are next. Here's why"** → CTA: Comment YOUR CITY
   *Why now:* Active crisis, infrastructure failure pattern across multiple cities
   *Pillar:* water_truth

4. **"Scientists just found a tree that filters 98% of microplastics. I tested it against my $11K system"** → CTA: Save this
   *Why now:* Viral HN story, chance to position ClearWave against "miracle" solutions
   *Pillar:* water_truth

5. **"North Carolina is spreading PFAS-contaminated sludge on your food. With zero regulations. Here's the map"** → CTA: Comment SLUDGE
   *Why now:* Breaking NC story, food supply fear, regulatory gap outrage
   *Pillar:* water_truth

## 🎬 Cross-niche format borrows

- **Grant Cardone's "I'm broke anyway" transparency hook** — Adapt to: "I did $138K last month and I'm still scared every day. Here's why that's good." Vulnerability + success = engagement. Use for business pillar content.

- **Gary Vee "zero interest in stores" contrarian positioning** — Borrow framework: "Why I'll never sell ClearWave systems in Home Depot (and why that protects you)." Explain D2C model as customer advantage, not business limitation.

- **CNET's "biggest myth" format** — Deploy across water content: "The biggest myth about whole-home filters" / "...about water softeners" / "...about bottled water." Myth-busting = saves + authority.

---

**Watch next week:** FDA response to baby formula PFAS coverage, any municipal water crisis escalations, and Grant Cardone/Hormozi content format shifts (vulnerability trend may be peaking).
